item.title: Tomasi Cama steps down after disappointing season as All Blacks 7s coach
item.description: Tomasi Cama steps down after disappointing season as All Blacks 7s coach

item.title: Seruvakula embraces coaching challenge ahead of Flying Fijians campaign
item.description: It is undoubtedly the most challenging period of my coaching career, but I am ready to embrace whatever comes my way. Those were the words of Acting Fiji Water Flying Fijians Head Coach, Senirusi Seruvakula, speaking to fijivillage Sports.
item.content:encoded:
It is undoubtedly the most challenging period of my coaching career, but I am ready to embrace whatever comes my way.

Those were the words of Acting Fiji Water Flying Fijians Head Coach, Senirusi Seruvakula, speaking to fijivillage Sports.

Seruvakula says the players will assemble in the North as preparations for the Nations Championship continue, and he is confident the team will deliver a strong performance.

Speaking at the launch of the Vodafone Super Deans competition at Gospel High School, Seruvakula says this year’s Deans competition promises to be another exciting showcase of talent, and he is looking forward to seeing the next generation of Fiji rugby stars emerge.

Turning his attention to the Flying Fijians’ opening match against Wales, Seruvakula described the clash as a highly anticipated encounter and said the team will not take any opponent lightly.

Fiji will take on Wales on the July 4th at Cardiff City in Wales at 6.10am.

item.title: Tony Brown to jump ship from Springboks to All Blacks after RWC 2027
item.description: New Zealand Rugby (NZR) has confirmed that Tony Brown will leave his role with South Africa to join the All Blacks coaching setup

item.title: Fiji Men and Fijiana 7s finish sixth at Bordeaux 7s
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team finished their Bordeaux 7s campaign in sixth place after a 17-14 defeat to the United States in the fifth-place playoff.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team finished their Bordeaux 7s campaign in sixth place after a 17-14 defeat to the United States in the fifth-place playoff.

Fiji raced to a 14-0 lead in the first half, with Vuiviawa Naduvalo scoring a double, but were unable to maintain their advantage after the break.

The Americans responded strongly in the second half, crossing through David Still, Will Chevalier, and Lucas Lacamp to secure the win.

Fiji ended the World Rugby Sevens Championship in sixth place on 36 points.

The Fijiana 7s side also finished sixth at the Bordeaux 7s after falling 24-12 to France in their fifth-place playoff match.

They finished in seventh place with 26 points on the Championship standings.

Both teams have also qualified for the 2026/2027 World Rugby 7s Series.

Meanwhile, host France defeated New Zealand 14-5 to claim the men's title, and Australia beat New Zealand 26-19 to win the women's division.

item.title: Naduvalo and Veilawa named in Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 After impressive performances throughout the 2025/2026 World Rugby SVNS Series, Fiji Airways Men's 7s representatives Vuiviawa Naduvalo and Terio Veilawa have been named in the Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

After impressive performances throughout the 2025/2026 World Rugby SVNS Series, Fiji Airways Men's 7s representatives Vuiviawa Naduvalo and Terio Veilawa have been named in the Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year.

Also included in the Dream Team are South Africa's Tristan Leyds and Shilton van Wyk, Australia's Harry Hutchison, Spain's Jeremy Trevithick, and Argentina's Luciano González.

Leyds was also named Men's SVNS Player of the Year, while New Zealand's Kele Lasaqa received the Men's SVNS Rookie of the Year award.

item.title: Panthers crush Wests Tigers 68-0
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 The Penrith Panthers continued their dominance in the NRL with a 68-0 thrashing of the Wests Tigers.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

The Penrith Panthers continued their dominance in the NRL with a 68-0 thrashing of the Wests Tigers.

The Panthers ran in 12 unanswered tries, with talisman Nathan Cleary contributing 24 points through a try and nine conversions.

Brian To'o, Freddy Lussick, and Thomas Jenkins each scored doubles, while Paul Alamoti, Isaah Yeo, Jack Cogger, Izack Tago, and Lindsay Smith also crossed for tries.

The Panther lead the points table with 26 points.

item.title: Declan Rice named England vice-captain for FIFA World Cup
item.description: Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice has been named England's vice-captain ahead of the FIFA World Cup. England coach Thomas Tuchel confirms Rice's role following Saturday's friendly win over New Zealand, saying the midfielder would serve as deputy to captain Harry Kane.
item.content:encoded:
Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice has been named England's vice-captain ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

England coach Thomas Tuchel confirms Rice's role following Saturday's friendly win over New Zealand, saying the midfielder would serve as deputy to captain Harry Kane.

The 27-year-old has earned 72 caps for England and is expected to be one of the team's most experienced players at the tournament.

Rice recently helped Arsenal win the Premier League title for the first time since the 2003/04 season and also played a key role in the club's run to the UEFA Champions League final.

Tuchel reveals he informed Rice of the leadership role earlier this year when the midfielder captained England during a friendly against Wales in Kane's absence.

Rice has now joined England's training camp in the United States alongside Arsenal teammates Bukayo Saka, Noni Madueke and Eberechi Eze as preparations continue for the World Cup.

England will begin their World Cup campaign against Croatia on June 17 and will also face Ghana and Panama in Group L.

item.title: Lionel Messi prepares for sixth FIFA World Cup
item.description: A different Lionel Messi could be on show as the Argentine star prepares for his sixth FIFA World Cup. The 38-year-old, who made his Barcelona debut as a dazzling teenage winger in 2003, has transformed his game over the years to remain among football's elite players.
item.content:encoded:
A different Lionel Messi could be on show as the Argentine star prepares for his sixth FIFA World Cup.

The 38-year-old, who made his Barcelona debut as a dazzling teenage winger in 2003, has transformed his game over the years to remain among football's elite players.

Former Barcelona coaches Frank Rijkaard and Pep Guardiola gradually shifted Messi from the wing into a more central role, where he became the team's main creator and attacking force.

Messi has represented Argentina at five FIFA World Cups, including Germany 2006, South Africa 2010, Brazil 2014, Russia 2018 and Qatar 2022.

Now relying more on vision, passing and positioning than pace, Messi continues to influence matches at the highest level more than two decades after his professional debut.

item.title: Yengi scores on debut as Socceroos hold Switzerland 1-1
item.description: Tete Yengi scored on his debut as Australia held Switzerland to a 1-all draw in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cu
